danger_signals.visual_form: Species disappear from evolutionary tree display; biome indicators collapse
danger_signals.meaning: Critical ecosystem failure; player intervention needed to restore balance

danger_signals.visual_form: Ice cap indicators recede; sea level rise shown on planetary map
danger_signals.meaning: Temperature-dependent disaster state; oceans may boil away if unchecked

danger_signals.visual_form: Atmospheric gas panel shows toxic composition shifts; disease outbreak notifications
danger_signals.meaning: Population-dependent disaster threshold approaching; civilization health degrading

danger_signals.visual_form: Atmospheric dust increase; temperature drop spike on climate readout
danger_signals.meaning: Nuclear conflict has occurred; civilization in acute survival crisis

danger_signals.visual_form: Dust layer in atmosphere panel increases; global temperature drops visibly
danger_signals.meaning: Natural disaster has occurred; climate system destabilized temporarily

danger_signals.visual_form: Earthquake notification paired with coastal water surge on terrain map
danger_signals.meaning: Seismic event has triggered secondary water-based disaster

danger_signals.visual_form: Weather disaster notification; atmospheric instability markers appear
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental system in active distress; potential damage to lifeforms/civilization

danger_signals.visual_form: Terrain display shows burning biome cells; vegetation loss markers
danger_signals.meaning: Heat-dependent disaster; ecosystem damage in progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Omega (Ω) unit counter near zero; control panel greys out unavailable tools
danger_signals.meaning: Player intervention capacity severely limited; planet development stalled

safety_signals.visual_form: Atmospheric gas percentages within optimal range on display panel
safety_signals.meaning: Planetary conditions support multicellular life; ecosystem stability maintained

safety_signals.visual_form: Global temperature gauge shows middle-range values; ice caps present but not expanding
safety_signals.meaning: Climate suitable for diverse life forms; no immediate thermal crisis

safety_signals.visual_form: Evolutionary tree displays multiple thriving taxa; biome indicators show variety
safety_signals.meaning: Ecosystem health good; evolutionary pressure balanced

safety_signals.visual_form: City development indicators show progression through technology eras; infrastructure present on map
safety_signals.meaning: Sentient life achieving complexity; advancement toward space exodus goal

safety_signals.visual_form: Ocean coverage visible on planetary surface; water percentage in atmospheric display
safety_signals.meaning: Essential life-support resource present; habitability maintained

safety_signals.visual_form: Absence of disease outbreak notifications; population health indicators stable
safety_signals.meaning: Population conditions healthy; no epidemic-level crisis

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: City markers on planetary map; civilization development stage badges
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Destroyed cities appear as ruins; nuclear-devastated zones show radiative markers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Settlements cluster in habitable zones; terraforming equipment indicates species territory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Appears only after Nanotech Age city destroyed by nuclear explosion; distinct from organic life markers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Emerges in post-apocalyptic zones; replaces organic civilization visually

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mentioned as sapient example in screenshots; shown in evolutionary tree as distinct taxa
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupy marine/aquatic biomes; visible in biome distribution displays

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Listed as example taxa with equal evolutionary potential; shown in biodiversity indicators
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupy oceanic environments; represented in water biome layers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Prokaryote/eukaryote indicators visible; minimal biome coverage; evolutionary tree sparse
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reproduction rates low; mutation rates low; minimal ecosystem complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Taxa list expands; biome colors fill planetary map progressively; biodiversity meter rises
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Species diversify across multiple taxa; extinction risk decreases; ecosystem stabilizes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: City markers appear on map; technology era badges display (progressing toward Nanotech); population indicators rise
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sentient species organize settlements; technology advances; civilization exerts environmental control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple cities across planet; highest technology indicators active; infrastructure network visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Civilization manages planetary resources; technological spread accelerates; exodus preparation phase begins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sun becomes red giant; all life indicators zero; planetary surface becomes uninhabitable visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All lifeforms extinct; civilization ceased; planet rendered lifeless and barren

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nuclear winter markers active; atmospheric dust spike; temperature collapse; devastated zone markers on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Civilization fractured; survival-mode behavior; potential machine life emergence post-destruction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Machine life markers spread; organic biome coverage diminishes; nanotech signatures dominate map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must intervene with flood or other tools; organic life under existential threat; technological takeover visible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single biome type (daisies) covers planet; daisy color shifts relative to temperature on display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Daisies regulate temperature through color-based albedo changes; Gaia hypothesis mechanism demonstrated visually

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Terraforming equipment visible on map; atmospheric gas panel shows gradual composition shift; temperature/pressure indicators move toward habitability range
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Equipment actively transforms planetary conditions; slow drift toward life-supporting parameters; visual transformation of sterile world

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Omega (Ω) unit counter near depletion; interface panels show reduced functionality; tool availability dimmed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player control severely restricted; planetary development slows; only essential interventions possible

environmental_storytelling.technique: Atmospheric composition display as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Player reads oxygen/CO2/other gas percentages to three decimal places; compositional changes reflect both natural evolution and environmental crises (oxygen generators raising O2, pollution spikes increasing toxins)

environmental_storytelling.technique: Evolutionary tree visualization as species narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Radiate lifeforms shown branching from early animals; cetaceans shown as distinct taxa; all multicellular animals on equal evolutionary footing; tree expansion/contraction tells story of ecosystem health

environmental_storytelling.technique: Planetary surface map as civilization/ecosystem distribution story
environmental_storytelling.example: City clusters show where sentient species settled; biome colors show which lifeforms dominate regions; terraforming equipment placement marks player intervention zones; devastated zones tell story of past conflicts

environmental_storytelling.technique: Technology era badges as civilization progress narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Cities display progression through development stages; Nanotech Age represents pinnacle; visual difference between early and advanced settlements shows technological advancement story

environmental_storytelling.technique: Disaster notifications and aftermath as event narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Volcanic eruption increases atmospheric dust visually, lowering temperature; nuclear war creates nuclear winter effect; tsunami forms from earthquake in water; each disaster leaves visual trace of causal chain

environmental_storytelling.technique: Population health indicators as societal wellbeing story
environmental_storytelling.example: Disease outbreak notifications paired with population visualization; pollution accumulation shown in atmosphere before manifesting as health crisis; narrative of civilization degradation told through degrading indicators

environmental_storytelling.technique: Ice cap presence/absence as climate narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Ice caps recede with global warming; boil away completely in extreme heat scenarios; reformed by ice meteor impact; visual shorthand for planetary temperature story across geological time

environmental_storytelling.technique: Energy budget as player agency/planetary wealth narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Higher development planets support more Omega units; difficulty level affects budget; depletion forces strategic choices; tells story of civilization resource constraints

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Reproduce at baseline rates; mutation occurs slowly; occupy simple biomes; minimal environmental influence

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Established ecosystem (billions of years into planet)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Thrive or die based on environmental conditions; respond to temperature, oxygen, water availability; can cause mass extinction if ecosystem balance disrupted
Form cities; construct infrastructure; begin technology progression; show visual clustering in habitable zones
Manage planetary resources; construct terraforming equipment; potentially trigger nuclear war if fuel shortage occurs; prepare for planetary exodus
Civilization fragments; cities may be destroyed; potential transformation into machine life if nanotech city destroyed by nuclear explosion
Change color (albedo) relative to temperature; regulate global climate as temperature changes; demonstrate Gaia hypothesis feedback mechanism visually
Appears only after Nanotech civilization destroyed by nuclear explosion; replaces organic life; spreads across devastated zones; represents technological takeover narrative
Maintains atmospheric balance; supports diverse life; climate remains within habitability range; evolutionary progress continues
Species disappear rapidly; biome coverage collapses; atmospheric composition becomes hostile; recovery requires player intervention or long recovery period
All life ceases; oceans boil or freeze; planet becomes visually barren; no recovery possible

notes: SimEarth communicates world-state primarily through numerical displays (atmospheric percentages, temperature gauges, population metrics) combined with visual map representation (biome colors, city markers, ice cap extent). Danger is expressed as threshold-crossing: gas compositions outside safe ranges, temperatures too extreme, disease/pollution accumulation. Safety is the inverse—balanced conditions and diversified, thriving biomes. The game lacks faction conflict in traditional sense; instead, different lifeforms represent different evolutionary 'paths,' all competing for planetary resources. Political situations emerge implicitly through civilization development and potential nuclear conflict. The Gaia hypothesis mechanic (Daisyworld) is the primary 'political' system—lifeforms collectively regulate planetary conditions. Disasters communicate causality visually: volcanic eruption → dust → temperature drop. The energy budget system is the interface for player power and planetary 'wealth.' Machine life Easter egg represents a hidden post-human world-state achievable only through specific destruction sequence.
